name: Bus-B-Q
recent_news: No public news or web presence found; listing reflects a small Long Beach-based operation with ~6 employees and ~$301K revenue. Name 'Bus-B-Q' is unusual and may be a small charter/specialty operator rather than a contracted school-bus carrier.
fit_notes: Too small and opaque to be a meaningful tuck-in; would only matter as an asset/route pickup in LA basin. Unverified as a true school-bus contractor.

name: Stockton School Transportation
recent_news: Manta/business directory lists company as established 2002, CA-incorporated, est. revenue ~$430K, ~14 employees; Cecil Tomlin identified as Director/Board Member. No recent M&A or news.
fit_notes: Very small, owner-led Central Valley operator in a Summit-active state (CA). Long tenure (~24 yrs) suggests possible succession opening but scale (sub-$0.5M) is too small for a stand-alone tuck-in; only attractive as a route bolt-on if Summit already operates in Stockton/SJ County.

name: Rochon's Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Norway-Vulcan Area Schools and other Dickinson County/UP MI districts (inferred)
recent_news: Long-standing UP Michigan family operator (Rochon family name on local street, MI corp filing #800601165). Provides daily school routes, field trips and extracurricular service. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Classic small family-owned rural UP operator with apparent multigenerational ownership — succession a plausible angle. Geography (Norway, MI / Dickinson County) is remote from Summit's denser MI footprint, limiting density synergies; only fits if Summit is building UP/Wisconsin-border presence.

name: Washington School Bus Lines
districts_or_customers: Knox County Schools (one of 360+ contracted-bus independent providers) and surrounding counties
recent_news: Knox County Schools relies on independent contractors operating 360+ buses; Washington is one of those owner-operator carriers. ZoomInfo profile lists 10-19 employees, owner Mark Washington. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Knox County's contractor-model market is a known consolidation theater (KTG and others). Washington is sub-scale on its own but could be part of a roll-up of multiple Knox County owner-operators; standalone economics too thin for a single tuck-in.

name: TFMC Team Preferred Bus
recent_news: No website, address, or public news identified. NC public-school transportation is overwhelmingly district-run (yellow-bus operations are state/district), so private contractor footprint is limited to charter/private schools.
fit_notes: Likely a micro charter/private-school shuttle operator rather than a contracted public school-bus carrier. NC's district-run model means tuck-in opportunity is structurally limited; not a priority target.
